---
format:
  typst:
    papersize: a0
    margin:
      x: 10cm
      y: 10cm
    mainfont: "Arial"
    fontsize: 32pt
---

```{=typst}
#set text(font: "Arial", size: 32pt)

#show heading.where(level: 1): it => block(
  text(font: "Arial", size: 96pt, weight: "bold", it.body),
  below: 12pt
)

#show heading.where(level: 2): it => block(
  text(font: "Arial", size: 44pt, weight: "regular", it.body),
  below: 24pt
)

#show heading.where(level: 3): it => block(
  text(font: "Arial", size: 40pt, weight: "bold", it.body),
  below: 40pt
)

#show figure.caption: it => text(font: "Arial", size: 32pt, it)
```

# _tximeta_ support for mixed reference sets

## Michael I. Love^1^, Charlotte Soneson^2^

^1^Department of Genetics, UNC-Chapel Hill, Chapel Hill, NC, US \
^2^Computational Biology Platform, Friedrich Miescher Institute for Biomedical Research, Basel, Switzerland

---

```{=typst}
#columns(3, gutter: 1.5cm)[
```

### Introduction

Lorem ipsum body text here. This will flow in the first column
at 32pt as set globally above.

### Methods

More body text in the methods section. You can include R code
output, figures, and tables here as normal.

figure...

### Results

Results text here. Figures placed inside the columns block will
be constrained to the column width automatically.

figure...

### Discussion

Discussion text spanning into the third column. Keep adding
content and Typst will flow it across columns automatically.

### Conclusions

- Bullet one
- Bullet two
- Bullet three

### References

::: {#refs}
:::

```{=typst}
]
```
